Home/Best Freight Forwarders in Jamaica 2026: Mailpac vs ShipMe vs Aeropost vs ESS
Guide
Mar 20, 2026•10 min read

Best Freight Forwarders in Jamaica 2026: Mailpac vs ShipMe vs Aeropost vs ESS

If you shop on Temu, AliExpress, Amazon US, or any other international retailer, a freight forwarder is not optional — it is essential. Shipping directly to a Jamaican address is almost always prohibitively expensive and unreliable. A Miami-based freight forwarder gives you a US address, receives your packages, consolidates them, and flies them to Kingston or Montego Bay at a fraction of direct shipping costs. But not all forwarders are equal. Here is a detailed breakdown of Jamaica's four most popular options.

What is a Freight Forwarder and How Does It Work?

A freight forwarder maintains a physical warehouse in Miami, Florida (or occasionally in another US city). When you register, they assign you a unique suite or mailbox number at their Miami address. You use this as your "US shipping address" when shopping online. Once a package arrives at their warehouse, you are notified. You then either wait for more packages to consolidate (saving on per-shipment handling fees) or request immediate dispatch. The forwarder ships a consolidated load on a weekly or bi-weekly chartered flight to Manley International Airport (Kingston) or Sangster International Airport (Montego Bay), where you collect it or pay for local delivery.

Mailpac Group: Best Overall for Reliability

Mailpac is widely considered the most established freight forwarder in Jamaica, having operated for over 20 years. Their rates hover around US$7-8 per pound for air freight, with a minimum charge of 1 lb. They offer both Kingston and Montego Bay pickup points, plus a home delivery option for an additional fee. Their online platform is the most polished of the four, with real-time package tracking and clear invoicing. The main drawback is that their rates are not the cheapest, and their customer service can be slow during the Q4 holiday rush. They are best for shoppers who prioritise consistency and reliability over getting the absolute lowest price.

ShipMe: Best for Heavy or High-Volume Shoppers

ShipMe (operated by Supreme Ventures Logistics) has aggressively positioned itself as the value option in the Jamaican freight market. Their air freight rates are typically slightly below Mailpac's, and they frequently run promotions for reduced rates on new customer sign-ups. ShipMe is particularly strong for high-weight shipments because they have negotiated airline cargo rates that let them pass savings on to customers who ship heavier consolidated loads. They also offer a sea freight option for truly heavy or oversized items (furniture, appliances) at dramatically lower per-pound rates, though sea transit takes 2-4 weeks versus 3-5 days for air.

Aeropost: Best for Amazon US and Name-Brand Retailers

Aeropost operates across 40+ countries in Latin America and the Caribbean, making it the most internationally experienced forwarder on this list. Their Jamaican operation is solid, and their Miami address is accepted by virtually every US retailer, including those that specifically restrict forwarding addresses (some retailers block known forwarder addresses, but Aeropost's residential-style suites often bypass these blocks). They charge a small insurance fee automatically, which can add up, but is valuable for high-value electronics. Aeropost also offers a "Tiered Express" service for truly urgent packages. However, their rates per pound tend to be on the higher end of the market.

ESS (Express Shipping Solutions): Best for Montego Bay Residents

ESS has built a loyal customer base in western Jamaica, particularly in Montego Bay, St. James, Westmoreland, and Hanover. Their direct Montego Bay office and pickup location means western Jamaica residents do not have to travel to Kingston to collect packages, which is a genuine logistical advantage. Their rates are competitive, typically matching Mailpac's per-pound pricing. Their volume is lower than the larger players, which means packages are sometimes held longer waiting for a consolidated flight load. For Kingston-based shoppers, there is no particular advantage over Mailpac or ShipMe.

Hidden Fees to Watch Out For

All four forwarders have fees beyond the basic per-pound freight rate that can significantly impact your final cost. Handling fees of US$1-3 per package are charged at the Miami warehouse for receiving each individual box. Package consolidation may be free or carry a small charge. Storage fees begin accumulating after your packages sit in the Miami warehouse beyond a set window (usually 30 days). Customs brokerage fees apply if your shipment exceeds the US$100 threshold and the forwarder has to file a formal import declaration on your behalf — these can range from J$1,500 to J$5,000 per entry. Always read the full fee schedule on any forwarder's website before choosing one.

The Bottom Line: Which Forwarder Should You Choose?

For most Jamaican online shoppers who are primarily using Temu and AliExpress for smaller packages, Mailpac remains the safest default choice due to its track record, Jamaica-wide coverage, and polished online interface. If you are a high-volume shopper or regularly ship heavier items, compare ShipMe's rate card before your next large order — the per-pound savings can add up to hundreds of dollars a year. If you live in Montego Bay or western Jamaica, ESS deserves serious consideration simply for the collection convenience.
